data.narrative:
Heven AeroTech, a Series B-stage frontier technology company in Defense & Security founded by Bentzion Levinson, is currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $100M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of ten comparable companies: two public-market references (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private companies including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the last post-money valuation of $1.0B, yielding an implied value of $1.9B. Peer-median methodology identifies six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B and a range spanning $300M to $3.0B. This range reflects meaningful heterogeneity within the cohort, with Dream Security at the high end ($3.0B) and unnamed peers clustered closer to Heven's current mark.

The two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $956M (low), $1.5B (median), and $2.2B (high). The current $1.0B valuation sits near the lower bound, consistent with peer-median positioning but below the stage-progression step-up. Key uncertainties include whether Heven's operational metrics justify positioning toward the upper quartile of peer valuations and the degree to which the heterogeneous peer range—spanning a 10x spread—reflects genuine business fundamentals versus sector-wide valuation volatility.
